metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test (e.g. in the test suite or as a CI shell step) that greps `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md` for the required strings (fire-rate query phrase, `'0.6'`, `'0.7'`, recording-location marker) and fails if any are absent, then list this test explicitly in Scope's test bullet.
findings.finding: AC4 asserts the `g7-promotion-path.md` file is 'CI-verifiable' but the Scope's test bullet enumerates no test that checks the file's content; 'proven by grepping it' reads as a manual step, making the 'CI-verifiable' claim unsupported and the content AC unenforceable by automated tooling.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4; Scope — tests bullet
findings.suggested_fix:
Add to Scope tests bullet: 'docs: g7-promotion-path.md content check (grep for fire-rate query, 0.6/0.7 literals, recording-location marker)' and add a pytest test like:
```python
def test_g7_promotion_path_content():
    text = Path('docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md').read_text()
    assert 'REVIEW_RESULT' in text  # fire-rate query
    assert '0.6' in text
    assert '0.7' in text
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py` to the Scope and update `_validate_routing_entry` to validate the new `require_parent_id` boolean axis in `applies_at`.
findings.finding: `src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py` (specifically `_validate_routing_entry`, lines 131-213) is the actual `applies_at` schema validator, but it is absent from the plan's Scope section. The plan says 'add the `require_parent_id` axis to the `applies_at` schema' and calls out `registry.py` and `validate_packaged_routing()`, but `_validate_routing_entry` — which enforces the applies_at structure — lives in the shared criteria layer (`criteria/overlay.py`) and is only re-exported into registry.py. Without updating `_validate_routing_entry`, a malformed or misspelled `require_parent_id` value in `criteria_routing.json` silently passes the CI parity gate.
findings.location: Scope section — 'add the `require_parent_id` axis to the applies_at schema + `applies()`'; src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py:131-213
findings.suggested_fix: In `criteria/overlay.py:_validate_routing_entry`, after the existing `scope` check (line ~212), add: `rpi = ap.get('require_parent_id'); if rpi is not None and not isinstance(rpi, bool): raise CriteriaError(...)`. Also add `criteria/overlay.py` to the Scope list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the G7 agent learns its own ticket_id (e.g., inject it as a literal in the rubric instructions via a minimal extra_context block in pass1_chunk, or document an alternative first-hop mechanism), reconciling the 'NO new context plumbing' constraint with the need to identify the ticket under review.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the G7 agent can call `show_ticket(<own_ticket_id>)` as the first hop of its two-hop parent read, but `pass1_chunk()` does not inject the ticket_id into the agent's context — the agent receives only the plan text (via `{{plan}}`) and the rubric instructions, neither of which reliably contains the ticket_id. The claim 'the agent knows its OWN ticket_id' is unverified against the actual call path.
findings.location: What section — 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY'; passes.py:pass1_chunk (lines 281–345); plan_review_finder.md ({{plan}} is the only injected variable)
findings.suggested_fix: The lightest-weight resolution consistent with 'no new context plumbing': add the ticket_id as a one-line prefix to the `extra_context` string already threaded to `_pass1_with_ladder` for G7's chunk — parallel to how G5's decomposition state is injected. This requires a small targeted change to `run_pass1` (inject `ctx.ticket_id` for G7-bearing chunks) rather than a full `_assemble_context_uncached` change. Alternatively, revise the rubric to instruct the agent to search for the parent_id in `ctx.state` directly rather than via a two-hop call.

findings.finding: AC4 requires the committed `g7-promotion-path.md` file to contain 'the exact query to compute G7's fire-rate + validity from the REVIEW_RESULT sidecars' but specifies no query language, format, or schema anchor for the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar. The grep-based CI check verifies presence of '0.6', '0.7', and the recording location, but does not verify that the query is actually executable or targets the correct sidecar field. An executor who writes a prose description of the query (rather than a runnable command) would pass the grep check but leave the fire-rate observability path broken.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 ('the exact query to compute G7's fire-rate + validity from the REVIEW_RESULT sidecars'); What section — promotion-path.md description

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct AC5's rationale: replace 'AGENT_TIER membership is what grants the show_ticket tool (registry.py:62-65)' with the accurate claim — the pydantic_ai runner grants show_ticket to every AGENT exec-mode criterion (all criteria with exec:AGENT in the routing entry, per runner.py:239-244); AGENT_TIER registration is needed for exec_tier() dispatch and cost-gating, not for the tool grant itself.
findings.finding: AC5 asserts 'AGENT_TIER membership is what grants the show_ticket tool (registry.py:62-65)' — but the code at that exact location says the OPPOSITE: 'NOTE: this is NOT a tool-capability boundary — agentic tooling (filesystem + rebar) is granted by a prompt's execution_mode, not per criterion-id'. The runner confirms this (runner.py:239-244): ALL pydantic_ai agents receive filesystem + rebar tools regardless of AGENT_TIER membership. The mechanism granting show_ticket to G7 is exec:AGENT routing (which maps to the pydantic_ai runner path), not AGENT_TIER registration per se.
findings.location: AC line: 'a test confirms G7 is registered in AGENT_TIER (NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED) — AGENT_TIER membership is what grants the show_ticket tool (registry.py:62-65)'
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC5 rationale to: 'a test confirms G7 is registered in AGENT_TIER (NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED) — AGENT_TIER registration is required for exec_tier() dispatch (orchestrator routes G7 as an agent loop) and cost-gating; the show_ticket tool is granted by the pydantic_ai runner to every exec:AGENT criterion (runner.py:239-244), not by AGENT_TIER set membership per the registry.py:62-65 comment.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name `src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py` (`_validate_routing_entry`, line 190-212) explicitly in the Scope as requiring a change to accept and validate the new `require_parent_id: bool` key in `applies_at`, alongside `registry.py`'s `applies()`.
findings.finding: The `require_parent_id` axis is a new `applies_at` schema key whose validation site (`_validate_routing_entry` in `criteria/overlay.py:131-212`) is not named as a file requiring change. The plan says 'validate_packaged_routing() and the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis' and names only registry.py's `applies()` + `applies_at` schema — but the structural validator that enforces applies_at key legality lives in `overlay.py:190-212`, not `registry.py`. If `_validate_routing_entry` is not updated, G7's routing entry either (a) fails the packaged-routing CI gate when it encounters an unknown key, or (b) is silently ignored if the validator only checks known keys — either way the new axis is never enforced at load time.
findings.location: Scope section ('add the `require_parent_id` axis to the applies_at schema + `applies()`') and src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py:190-212
findings.suggested_fix: Add `criteria/overlay.py` to the Scope file list and add an AC that `_validate_routing_entry` accepts `require_parent_id: bool` and rejects non-bool values with a located RegistryError.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify G7's error-handling contract for show_ticket failures: define whether an unreachable/missing parent causes the criterion to soft-pass (advisory skip with a logged warning), hard-fail with a surfaced error, or retry — and add an observability note (log/metric) for 'parent not found' events so operators can detect orphaned parent references.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that G7's agent calls `show_ticket(parent_id)` at runtime but is entirely silent on what happens when that call fails — e.g., the parent ticket is deleted, the ticket store is temporarily unavailable, or `parent_id` resolves to an inaccessible record. No retry, fallback, graceful-degradation mode (skip/advisory-pass), or error surface is specified for this new external dependency in the AGENT execution path.
findings.location: What section / Scope section: 'G7 is exec: AGENT … the agent FETCHES the parent at runtime … a two-hop read entirely within the always-available show_ticket tool'
findings.suggested_fix: In the rubric (plan_review_G7.md) and the Scope/AC, add: 'If show_ticket(parent_id) returns an error or empty result, emit a single advisory finding noting the parent was unreadable and return without scoring — do not hard-fail the gate. Log a warning with the ticket_id and parent_id for operator visibility.' This preserves the gate's availability while making failures observable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fallback protocol to the G7 rubric specification: when `show_ticket(<parent_id>)` returns an error string, G7 must ABSTAIN with a coverage note (not emit findings), mirroring the T13/E4 fail-open pattern.
findings.finding: No fallback is specified for the case where G7's agent cannot read the parent ticket via `show_ticket`. `show_ticket` in `pai_tools.py:194-204` returns a string like `"Error: <exc>"` on any read failure (best-effort, never raises). The rubric is directed to call `show_ticket(<parent_id>)` and compare scope, but no protocol is given for what the agent should do when the tool returns an error string — should it ABSTAIN (emit no findings), emit a coverage note, or fail closed?
findings.location: What section ('CONTEXT ASSEMBLY — … its rubric instructs it to call `show_ticket(<parent_id>)`') and src/rebar/llm/pai_tools.py:194-204
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the rubric description in the plan: 'If `show_ticket(<parent_id>)` returns an error string (starts with "Error:"), emit no findings and record ABSTAIN with reason "parent ticket unreadable" — never fabricate a parent plan.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command to AC3 — e.g. `grep -q 'parent wins\|PARENT WINS\|parent.*wins' src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_G7.md` and `grep -q 'parent.*realign\|realign.*parent' src/rebar/llm/passes.py` — and add these grep assertions to the test suite or as a dedicated content-check test.
findings.finding: AC3 ('The rubric and the Pass-4 coaching move state the parent-wins rule and direct remediation toward realigning the leaf or updating the parent FIRST — never silently diverging the leaf') has no concrete proving command; there is no grep or automated test anchoring the presence of the parent-wins text in the rubric file or passes.py.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC3: 'Verified by: `grep -qi "parent wins" src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_G7.md && grep -qi "update.*parent.*first\|parent.*first" src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_G7.md`; a test asserts these patterns are present.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py` to the Scope and specify that a `has_parent_id` property (mirroring `has_children`) will be added to `PlanContext`, sourcing from `ctx.state.get('parent_id')`.
findings.finding: `PlanContext` (defined in `det_floor.py`) currently has no `has_parent_id` attribute or property. The plan says to thread the ticket's `has_parent_id` fact into `route_criteria()` via the assembled `PlanContext`, but `det_floor.py` is not listed in the Scope section as a file to edit.
findings.location: Scope section — 'thread the ticket's has_parent_id fact through `route_criteria()`'; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py:94-129
findings.suggested_fix: Add to `PlanContext` in `det_floor.py`: `@property\ndef has_parent_id(self) -> bool: return bool(self.state.get('parent_id'))`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the parent-realign move's id (e.g. '13'), name, and `{subject}`-bearing template string in the plan (or in the rubric AC), so the implementer has an authored template that satisfies `validate_move_registry`'s strict schema.
findings.finding: The new Pass-4 coach move for parent-realign is required by the plan ('the coach MOVE_REGISTRY (one parent-realign move in passes.py)') but its move id, name, and template string are never specified. `validate_move_registry` (review_kernel/coach.py:63-96) strictly enforces that every move has a non-empty `name` and a `template` containing exactly one `{subject}` placeholder — a move without these raises `ValueError` at load time (strict=True). An implementer has no authored template to write.
findings.location: Scope section ('the coach MOVE_REGISTRY (one parent-realign move in passes.py)') and src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:729-792 (MOVE_REGISTRY)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope or AC #3: 'Move id "13", name "realign to parent", template: "Realign {subject} to the parent ticket's declared scope, or update the parent first if the parent is wrong."'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state that `require_parent_id` defaults to `false` when absent (preserving existing behavior), add this default to the applies_at schema spec in the plan, and confirm that `validate_packaged_routing()` treats its absence as `false` — no existing entries need editing.
findings.finding: The plan adds a new boolean axis `require_parent_id` to the `applies_at` schema consumed by `applies()` and `validate_packaged_routing()`, but never states the default value (True/False/absent=ignore) for existing routing entries that do not include this field, leaving the backward-compatibility of every existing criterion entry unspecified.
findings.location: What section — ROUTING paragraph; Scope — registry.py applies_at schema bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the ROUTING paragraph: 'Default for existing entries that omit require_parent_id is false (field is optional; absence means no parent-id requirement). validate_packaged_routing() must accept entries lacking this field.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact function-signature change: either (a) add a `has_parent_id: bool` property to `PlanContext` and a matching `has_parent_id: bool` parameter to `registry.applies()`, or (b) name another concrete mechanism — so the threading is unambiguous and type-checkable.
findings.finding: The protocol for threading `has_parent_id` from `PlanContext` into `registry.applies()` is never specified at the schema/function-signature level. The plan says 'thread the ticket's `has_parent_id` fact through `route_criteria()` (orchestrator.py:337-375, which calls `registry.applies()`)' but does not say (a) whether this requires a new `has_parent_id: bool` property on `PlanContext` (currently only `has_children` exists as a computed property), (b) a new `has_parent_id: bool` parameter on `applies()`, or (c) reading `ctx.state.get('parent_id')` inline in `route_criteria`. These are three structurally different implementations with different type-check, test, and backward-compat implications.
findings.location: What section ('MECHANISM … add a new boolean axis `require_parent_id` … thread the ticket's `has_parent_id` fact through `route_criteria()`') and src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py:94-133 (PlanContext), src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:311-339 (applies())
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'Add `has_parent_id: bool` as a computed property on `PlanContext` (reading `bool(self.state.get("parent_id"))`) and add a matching `has_parent_id: bool = False` keyword parameter to `registry.applies()`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC5) that verifies `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` is regenerated and contains a G7 entry — e.g., 'CI grep for "G7" in docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md passes'.
findings.finding: The Scope section lists 'the regenerated docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md' as a deliverable, but no AC verifies that this file is regenerated or correct — it is an orphaned described deliverable with no covering criterion.
findings.location: Scope section (last bullet); Acceptance Criteria (all seven ACs)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` is regenerated and contains a G7 entry (CI grep for "G7" in the file passes).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend _validate_routing_entry (overlay.py) to validate applies_at.require_parent_id as a boolean when present, consistent with how applies_at.scope is validated — the plan says the validator 'must accept' the new axis but the current code would silently accept any value without type enforcement.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the applies_at schema validator (overlay.py:_validate_routing_entry) 'must accept the new require_parent_id axis', implying it will validate the new field. The actual validator (overlay.py:190-212) only validates the 'scope' key's values; any unknown key in applies_at passes through silently without type checking. A malformed require_parent_id (e.g. a string or list instead of a boolean) in a routing entry would not be caught by the validator — it would silently reach applies() at runtime and evaluate as truthy/falsy in Python, potentially firing G7 unexpectedly.
findings.location: What section: 'validate_packaged_routing() and the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a short developer-contract note (in CLAUDE.md, a docs/plan-review-criteria-guide section, or a new ADR) documenting the `applies_at` schema extension point — specifically that `require_parent_id: true` is available as a routing axis and when to use it — so future criterion authors can discover and reuse this pattern.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new `require_parent_id` boolean axis on the `applies_at` schema — an extension to the routing contract that future criterion authors and maintainers of registry.py/orchestrator.py must know about — but no CLAUDE.md, ADR, or developer-facing design-doc update is planned to document this new schema capability. The regenerated criteria guide covers criterion *behavior*, not the *routing schema contract*.
findings.location: Scope section — registry.py applies_at schema + orchestrator.py route_criteria(); also Scope: 'validate_packaged_routing() and the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Routing Schema Reference' subsection to docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md (already being regenerated) that enumerates the `applies_at` axes (`scope`, `suppress_types`, `suppress_when`, `require_parent_id`), their semantics, and a one-liner on when each should be used. This keeps the contract colocated with the criteria guide and requires no separate ADR.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `require_parent_id` in `applies_at` is justified over having the G7 rubric's agent self-check for a parent via `show_ticket(own_id)` and return not-applicable early — if the agent self-check suffices, drop the schema axis and the `applies()` / `_validate_routing_entry` changes entirely.
findings.finding: The new `require_parent_id` boolean axis in `applies_at` is a premature abstraction: it has exactly one consumer (G7) at launch, and the AGENT already self-determines parent presence via its own `show_ticket(own_id)` call (which the rubric already instructs). The axis adds schema changes to `applies()`, `_validate_routing_entry`, and the routing JSON with no Rule-of-Three justification.
findings.location: Scope section — registry.py `applies_at` schema + `applies()` signature; What section — 'MECHANISM: add a new boolean axis `require_parent_id`'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the `require_parent_id` axis from the plan. Instead, instruct the G7 rubric to: (1) call `show_ticket(own_ticket_id)` to retrieve `parent_id`; (2) if `parent_id` is absent or null, emit zero findings and note 'not applicable — no parent'. This produces identical routing behavior with zero schema change, zero `applies()` signature change, and zero `_validate_routing_entry` update.

findings.finding: AC5 contradicts the Scope section on CODEBASE_GROUNDED membership: Scope explicitly says 'NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED', but AC5 says 'a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion'. CODEBASE_GROUNDED is `frozenset({"E4", "G1G2", "A1", "G6"})` (registry.py:66) and is what `plan_review_grounding` uses to decide whether to run the expensive agentic verifier (workflow_ops.py:229-230). If G7 is added to CODEBASE_GROUNDED, every G7 finding will trigger the agentic verifier even though G7 grounds against the ticket store, not code — and the Scope section correctly notes it should NOT be in CODEBASE_GROUNDED for exactly this reason.
findings.location: Scope section ('register G7 in CANONICAL_LLM + AGENT_TIER (NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED)') vs. AC5 ('a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion')
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC5 from 'G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion' to 'G7 is registered in CANONICAL_LLM and AGENT_TIER (but NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED), so it receives the show_ticket tool via its AGENT exec mode without triggering the agentic verifier for its findings.'

findings.finding: AC #4 contradicts the plan body on CODEBASE_GROUNDED membership. AC #4 states 'a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion (so it gets the tool),' but the plan body explicitly says G7 should NOT be in CODEBASE_GROUNDED ('NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED — that set is for criteria whose VERIFY re-grounds against CODE; G7 grounds against the parent TICKET via the store'). CODEBASE_GROUNDED membership also controls whether `plan_review_grounding` in workflow_ops.py triggers the agentic verifier for G7 findings — so the AC wording, if followed literally, would add G7 to CODEBASE_GROUNDED and cause the agentic verifier to run for every G7 finding.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #4 vs. Scope section ('NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED' note)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion' in AC #4 with 'AGENT_TIER criterion (not CODEBASE_GROUNDED)' to match the Scope section's explicit parenthetical.

findings.finding: AC item 4 says 'a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion (so it gets the tool)' — contradicting the What section which explicitly says 'NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED.' Adding G7 to CODEBASE_GROUNDED is mechanically wrong: (a) tool access for AGENT-tier criteria comes from AGENT_TIER membership alone, not CODEBASE_GROUNDED (registry.py:62-65 comment confirms this); (b) the plan_review_grounding workflow op (workflow_ops.py:229-232) triggers the expensive AGENTIC verifier whenever any finding cites a CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion — adding G7 there forces agentic verification on every leaf-with-parent G7 finding, a cost amplification the plan does not intend; (c) CODEBASE_GROUNDED is explicitly for criteria 'whose job is to reason about the live codebase' (registry.py:60-66) — G7 reasons against the ticket store, not code.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria item 4 vs What section (AGENT_TIER/CODEBASE_GROUNDED paragraph)
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC#4 to: 'a test confirms G7 is in AGENT_TIER and CANONICAL_LLM but NOT in CODEBASE_GROUNDED (tool access comes from AGENT_TIER; CODEBASE_GROUNDED triggers the agentic verifier, which is not needed for a ticket-store-grounded criterion), and that its rubric file references show_ticket for reading the parent.'

findings.finding: AC #4 contradicts the Scope section on CODEBASE_GROUNDED membership: the AC says 'a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion (so it gets the tool)' but the Scope section explicitly states 'NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED'. The codebase confirms CODEBASE_GROUNDED = frozenset({"E4","G1G2","A1","G6"}) and its doc comment (registry.py:62-65) explains CODEBASE_GROUNDED is a SEMANTIC set (whose Pass-2 verification re-grounds against CODE), not a tool-capability boundary. An implementer writing the AC-driven test would assert G7 ∈ CODEBASE_GROUNDED — which is wrong — while the Scope section says the opposite. The correct test is G7 ∈ AGENT_TIER.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #4 vs Scope section ('NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED — that set is for criteria whose VERIFY re-grounds against CODE')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion' in AC #4 with 'AGENT_TIER criterion (not CODEBASE_GROUNDED)' and confirm the test asserts `'G7' in registry.AGENT_TIER and 'G7' not in registry.CODEBASE_GROUNDED`.

findings.finding: AC4's test oracle is self-contradictory: it directs a test to confirm G7 is registered as an 'AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion' so it gets the tool, but the Scope section explicitly states G7 must be in AGENT_TIER 'NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED.' A test author following AC4 literally could check CODEBASE_GROUNDED membership (wrong tier) and either lock in incorrect registration or write a tautologically-passing test against the wrong oracle — the classic self-authored-oracle anti-pattern.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4; cross-ref: Scope ('register G7 in CANONICAL_LLM + AGENT_TIER (NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED)')
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC4 to read: 'a test confirms G7 is registered in CANONICAL_LLM and AGENT_TIER (and NOT in CODEBASE_GROUNDED) — verifying it receives the show_ticket tool via execution_mode, not via the codebase-grounded tool grant — and the rubric file references reading the parent ticket via show_ticket.'

findings.finding: The plan states 'add G7 to the static `criteria:` batch list IF criteria are enumerated there', framing it as conditional. The criteria ARE enumerated in plan-review.yaml (lines 55-117): every criterion has a static `- prompt: plan-review-<ID> / when: ${{...}}` entry. Without a G7 entry in this YAML, the `plan_review_assemble_criteria` op will emit an `include_G7` boolean but the batch step has no slot to read it — G7 will never run even when `route_criteria()` includes it.
findings.location: Scope section: 'src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml (add G7 to the static `criteria:` batch list if criteria are enumerated there)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the Scope bullet to: 'src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml — add `- prompt: plan-review-G7 / when: \'${{ steps.assemble.outputs.include_G7 }}\'` to the static `criteria:` batch list (confirmed enumerated at lines 55-117).' Remove the 'if criteria are enumerated there' hedge.

findings.finding: The plan's Scope says 'add G7 to the static `criteria:` batch list **if criteria are enumerated there**' — but inspection of `plan-review.yaml` confirms criteria ARE statically enumerated (lines 55–117 list 30+ prompt entries each with a `when:` guard). The conditional 'if' makes this an optional edit in the plan text, but it is actually a required edit. Missing this entry means G7's `include_G7` output from `plan_review_assemble_criteria` has no corresponding `when:` slot in the batch, so the criterion will never fire in the v3 workflow engine despite routing correctly.
findings.location: Scope section: 'src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml (add G7 to the static criteria: batch list if criteria are enumerated there)'

findings.finding: AC5 labels G7 as 'an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion' for the purpose of the registration test, but the Scope section explicitly says G7 is NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED — it belongs only in AGENT_TIER (with a parenthetical explanation of why). An executing agent following AC5 literally would write a test asserting G7 appears in CODEBASE_GROUNDED, which would either fail (if the executor correctly uses AGENT_TIER) or produce a mis-registered criterion that silently grants it the wrong capabilities.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 ('a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion'); Scope section — 'register G7 in CANONICAL_LLM + AGENT_TIER (NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED — that set is for criteria whose VERIFY re-grounds against CODE)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC5's clause 'a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion (so it gets the tool)' with 'a test confirms G7 is registered in CANONICAL_LLM and in AGENT_TIER (not CODEBASE_GROUNDED) — which is the mechanism by which the pydantic_ai runner grants the show_ticket tool — and the rubric file references reading the parent ticket via show_ticket.'

findings.finding: AC4 says 'a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion (so it gets the tool)' but the Scope section explicitly says 'register G7 in CANONICAL_LLM + AGENT_TIER (NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED)' and the What section says 'exec: AGENT'. An implementer following the Scope/What would register G7 in AGENT_TIER only; an implementer following AC4 would also add it to CODEBASE_GROUNDED. These directly contradict each other.
findings.location: AC4 vs. Scope (registry.py paragraph) and What (exec: AGENT bullet)
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC4's test assertion to: 'a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT_TIER criterion (so it gets the show_ticket tool) and is NOT in CODEBASE_GROUNDED.'

findings.finding: AC4 states 'a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion (so it gets the tool)' — but the Scope section explicitly states 'register G7 in CANONICAL_LLM + AGENT_TIER (NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED — that set is for criteria whose VERIFY re-grounds against CODE; G7 grounds against the parent TICKET via the store)'. These two statements directly contradict each other; an implementer writing the test will not know which tier membership to assert.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC4 vs. Scope section (registry.py registration note)
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC4's parenthetical from '(so it gets the tool)' to align with the Scope's explicit NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED rationale: 'a test confirms G7 is registered in CANONICAL_LLM and AGENT_TIER (not CODEBASE_GROUNDED — tool access is granted by AGENT_TIER membership per registry.py:62-65) and the rubric references reading the parent ticket.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
